ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2006 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection, the court ordered case dismissed pursuant to CCP Sections 583.210 and 583.250. Proceedings reported by: Susan Lee, CSR# 4280 (212)

Notice Of Renewed Motion Of Defendant Linsey Freman To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons; Alternatively, For Delay In Prosecution
Off Calendar Oct-03-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael J. Veiluva of Alborg Veiluva & Epstein, LLP, 925-939-9880, appearing for defendant and moving party
Brian McCauley, of the Law Office of J. Brian McCauley, 974-1515, appearing for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LINSEY FREEMAN'S Renewed Motion Of Defendant To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons; Alternatively, For Delay In Prosecution is granted per CCP 583.250 or alternatively pursuant to CCP 420 (a)(2), CRC 372 for failure to timely serve or prosecute action. Order and Judgment signed in open Court. (301/PJB/CK) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189

Ntc Of ***Amended*** Hrg Date For Mo Of Deft Linsey Freeman To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Aug-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael J. Veiluva, of Alborg, Veiluva & Epstein, LLP, 925-939-9880, appearing for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LINSEY FREEMAN'S Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons, or alternatively, for delay in prosecution is denied. The proof of service is not valid. "Causing" service by mail is not proof of service. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
